message:
feat(core,abi): port /etc/protocols and /etc/networks parsers to core::resolv (bd-np-epic)

Lifts the inline line-parsing logic for protocol and network entries
out of resolv_abi.rs (parse_protocols_line, ~17 lines) and
unistd_abi.rs (parse_networks_line, ~25 lines including the
dotted-quad-or-decimal number parser) into pure-safe core::resolv,
joining parse_services_line and parse_hosts_line that already lived
there.

New core surface:
  pub struct ProtocolEntry { name, number, aliases }
  pub fn parse_protocols_line(line: &[u8]) -> Option<ProtocolEntry>
  pub fn lookup_protocol_by_name / by_number   (alias-aware,
                                                 case-insensitive)

  pub struct NetworkEntry  { name, number, aliases }
  pub fn parse_networks_line(line: &[u8]) -> Option<NetworkEntry>
  pub fn lookup_network_by_name / by_number    (alias-aware,
                                                 case-insensitive)
  pub fn parse_network_number(s: &str) -> Option<u32>

parse_network_number isolates the historical glibc inet_network()
behavior: plain decimal stays as-is (so `127` -> 127), but partial
dotted forms shift each octet into its big-endian slot (so
`127.0.0.0` -> 0x7f000000, `192.168` -> 0xc0a80000). Octets >255 and
>4 dotted parts are rejected.

abi changes:
  - getprotobyname / getprotobynumber call core lookup helpers
    directly, dropping their for-loop scans entirely.
  - parse_networks_line in unistd_abi.rs becomes a 4-line adapter
    that wraps core::resolv::parse_networks_line into the existing
    (Vec<u8>, u32) tuple consumed by 5 call sites (getnetent
    iterator, getnetbyname, getnetbyaddr, getnetbyname_r,
    getnetbyaddr_r) — call sites updated to pass &pname now that
    name is owned.

Verified:
  - 29 new core::resolv tests pass (8 protocol, 9 netnum, 12 network)
  - 173/173 abi lib tests pass
  - 178/178 unistd_abi integration tests pass
  - 61/61 resolv_abi integration tests pass

Closes bd-np-epic.
